steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to present the methods used in the 2020 study — specifically how the researchers conducted the study, not what they found. The notes identify two methods: tagging ducks with radio frequency ID trackers and recording their nest box visits.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'After tracking how many nest boxes the 138 wood ducks visited, the researchers found that the younger ducks tended to visit more nest boxes than the older ducks.': This focuses on a result (age-related behavior) rather than describing the methods. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The researchers investigated each nesting site for signs that it had been visited by the female wood ducks.': This describes a procedure not mentioned in the notes — the notes say trackers on the ducks recorded visits, not that researchers inspected each site manually. ✗
  • Choice C — 'By recording how many nest boxes each duck visited, the researchers discovered that only a relatively small percentage (13.0%) of the ducks visited 10 or more nest boxes.': This leads with a result (the 13.0% finding) rather than presenting the methods. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The researchers tagged 138 female wood ducks with radio frequency ID trackers and recorded how many nest boxes each duck visited during the nesting season.': Directly states both methods from the notes — tagging and recording — with no shift to results. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
